answer:
First, we need to find the magnitude (r) and angle (θ) of the given Cartesian coordinates.

Magnitude (r) = √(x² + y²)
r = √((-3)² + (5π/6)²)
r = √(9 + (5π/3)²)
r = √(9 + (25/3)π²)
r = √(9(1 + (5π/3)²))
r = 3√(1 + (5π/3)²)

Angle (θ) = arctan(y/x) + π/2 if x < 0
θ = arctan(5π/6 / (-3)) + π/2
θ = arctan(-1/2 - π/3) + π/2
θ = arctan(-(1/2 + π/3)) + π/2
θ = arctan(-(1/2 - π/3)) + π/2
θ = arctan(1/2 - π/3) + π
θ = π/2 + arctan(1/2 - π/3)
θ = π/2 + (-arctan(π/3 - 1/2) + π/2)
θ = π/2 + π/2 - arctan(π/3 - 1/2)
θ = π + arctan((1/2 - π/3) / (-1))
θ = π + arctan((3π/2 - 1) / 2)
θ = π + arctan(1.5π - 1 / 2)
θ = π + arctan(0.75π - 0.5)
θ = π + arctan(0.25π)
θ = π + (π/6)
θ = (5π/2)

So, the equivalent point in the standard polar coordinate representation is:
\boxed{(3\sqrt{1 + (5\pi/3)²}, \frac{5\pi}{2})}
